executedPreparation.command: dotnet publish src/OneDriveServerTransfer.App/OneDriveServerTransfer.App.csproj -c Release -r win-x64 --self-contained true -p:EnableWindowsTargeting=true -o artifacts/win-x64/m7-prepublish-check
executedPreparation.details: OneDriveServerTransfer.App.exe SHA-256 e09715d3e9aaa5b78ad99e5559c48f30f9e57b0ab9cb0cf69ebd8acd18ca4e1a; 302 files in the publish layout; bundled runtime pack Microsoft.NETCore.App.Runtime.win-x64 10.0.9. Generated under artifacts/win-x64, which is Git-ignored; the artifact is not committed.
limitations: The artifact was built on macOS and has NOT been executed on Windows; M7 acceptance requires execution on Windows Server 2019.

The local SDK produced bundled runtime 10.0.9 while Windows CI tests executed on runtime 10.0.10 (SDK rollForward latestPatch). The M7 executor should publish with the current supported .NET 10 servicing patch on or for the target environment and record the exact versions (D-039).

blockedAcceptanceChecks: Windows Server 2019 build execution and WPF startup
delegated Microsoft interactive sign-in with an authorized transfer account (WAM and system-browser fallback, MFA/Conditional Access)
real employee UPN resolution and real OneDrive root URL resolution, including proof both modes resolve to the same tenant, employee object, and drive ID
mandatory dry-run Scan against a real employee OneDrive
complete copy of real supported content
cancellation and resume; interruption and crash recovery
delta reconciliation, rename and move behavior, source deletion without local deletion
exclusive destination locking across processes or Windows sessions
disk-space reserve and controlled disk-full behavior on a real volume
path, reparse-point, hard-link, NTFS ACL, and exposure checks on real storage
report generation and operational review of real (sanitized) reports
temporary download URL expiration and credential isolation against live hosts
throttling, Range 206/200 behavior, source-hash and local SHA-256 behavior against the live tenant
production ACL and BitLocker (or approved equivalent/exception) validation
temporary Site Collection Administrator access removal, verification, and external recording
sign-out behavior on the target server
self-contained win-x64 publish acceptance with execution on Windows Server 2019
exactExternalBlockers: No Windows Server environment: server name, exact build, Desktop Experience confirmation, and execution account are all Not provided (docs/ENVIRONMENT_AND_INPUTS.md, Windows Server section).

No Microsoft 365 tenant inputs: tenant ID, Entra public-client Client ID, allowed OneDrive host, authorized transfer-account object ID list, admin consent, and redirect/WAM confirmations are all Not provided or Not confirmed (tenant section).

No controlled test employee OneDrive with the required content matrix (nested/empty folders, Unicode/Arabic/invalid/reserved/collision names, large file, package content, timestamp and hash cases, change/rename/move/delete cases, rejection inputs) (test data section).

No destination environment: local destination root, NTFS ACL baseline, BitLocker/equivalent/exception, free-space and controlled disk-full method, token-cache ACL, and network/TLS constraints are Not provided or Not confirmed.
This session executes on macOS with no remote access to any Windows environment or tenant; no credentials of any kind are available to it, and none may be invented.

ciEvidence.note: No application source changed on this branch; the last full Windows CI validation of the identical application source is the post-M6 hardening run 29999753949 (4 consecutive executions, 579/579) on commit afdc04852439a10b8081dc60a8cc8b404150a97d, integrated into main via PRs #17 and #18 (baseline 97a39fc1b06bf615b52470d8fc5677a7a254d245). The branch push run is recorded separately in the control files.

statusConclusion.completionLabel: Source Implementation Complete (unchanged; M7 must not be marked complete from preparation work)

unblockInstructions: Provide the inputs tracked in docs/ENVIRONMENT_AND_INPUTS.md: a Windows Server 2019 (Desktop Experience) environment with execution account and destination storage, the tenant and app-registration values, an authorized transfer account, and a controlled test employee OneDrive. Re-run M7 acceptance on that environment against the unchanged validated source.

redaction.note: No tenant identifiers, employee identities, or environment secrets exist in this evidence because none were provided.
